title: fix: fail closed on missing gpu backend token
author: its3li
pr_body_excerpt:
Fixes #11902

## Summary
- make GPU image backends reject generation requests when `PLN_GPU_TOKEN` is unset instead of allowing public access
- add the missing `x-backend-token` dependency to the SANA `/generate` endpoint
- keep `/health` available for LTX-2 health checks while protecting other routes

## Verification
- python -m py_compile image.pollinations.ai/z-image/server.py image.pollinations.ai/nunchaku/server.py image.pollinations.ai/image_gen_twinflow/server.py image.pollinations.ai/klein-runpod/handler.py image.pollinations.ai/sana/server.py image.pollinations.ai/video_gen_ltx2modal/ltx2-vastai/server.py image.pollinations.ai/video_gen_ltx2modal/ltx2-vastai/server_comfyui.py
- rg scan confirmed the old fail-open warning string is gone and remaining missing-token branches fail closed
- git diff --check -- image.pollinations.ai

gist.headline: Locking the garden gate
gist.blurb: We've tightened up the locks on our GPU backends. Now, generation requests require the proper tokens, keeping the hive's resources safe and sound.
gist.summary: Updated GPU image backends including SANA, LTX-2, and Twinflow to fail closed and reject generation requests when the PLN_GPU_TOKEN is missing.
gist.impact: This secures internal backend endpoints against unauthorized access while keeping health check routes open for monitoring.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a friendly security guard, locking a gate or securing a glowing server rack alongside the monitor robot.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny security guard hat, locking a small wooden gate in front of a glowing lime green (#ecf874) server rack. A chunky monitor robot stands nearby holding a pixelated golden key. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
